qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: User-manager role can self-promote to Trust and Safety via flag updates
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The finding covers self-assignment of the `ts` role through `PATCH /user/{user}` and unrestricted flag persistence, not the advisory's DPA creation flow where empty `evidence` is coerced to a falsey value and later rendered as a genuine self-deletion request.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: The only final qualifying finding is a role-escalation path in the user update workflow, while the advisory describes a DPA submission bug where an empty `evidence` value can make a third-party under-13 report appear to be a self-authored deletion request.
evidence: The advisory root cause is in `app/Http/Controllers/DPAController.php:68-76`, where the under-13 branch stores `underage` from `evidence`, and `resources/views/dpa/view.blade.php:67-73`, where a falsey `underage` value is rendered as `dpa-own-account`.
The DPA form distinguishes `own-removal` from `under-13` in `resources/views/dpa/new.blade.php:40-66`, with the `evidence` textarea shown only for the under-13 path described in the advisory.

The final qualifying finding instead targets `app/Policies/UserPolicy.php:58-65`, `app/Http/Controllers/UserController.php:41-42`, `resources/views/user/view.blade.php:73-94`, and `app/Models/User.php:147-156`, which together allow a `user-manager` to persist the `ts` flag onto their own account.
